factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with growth observed in Onsite locations and key accounts, particularly those opened recently, while manufacturing end markets and construction/reseller customers are experiencing weaker demand.

evidence.text: Demand is slowing, particularly in manufacturing end markets.

We experienced higher unit sales during the period that contributed to the increase in net sales in the period. This was primarily due to growth at our Onsite locations, with the strongest contribution from those sites opened in the last two years and a more modest contribution from more mature locations. This more than offset the impact of softer end market demand on our manufacturing customers and lower revenues to construction and reseller customers.

We experienced higher unit sales in the third quarter of 2023 that was primarily due to growth at our Onsite locations, particularly those opened in the last two years. This more than offset the impact of softer end market demand on our manufacturing customers and lower revenues to construction and reseller customers.

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditure outlook is mixed. While capital expenditures are expected to decrease due to deferred distribution center projects, there are signs of investment in information technology and distribution, indicating confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: For the full year of 2023, we expect our investment in property and equipment, net of proceeds from sales, to be within a range of $180.0 to $190.0. This is a decline from our prior range of $210.0 to $230.0 reflecting a deferral of several distribution center-related projects.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

evidence.text: We had an increase in our distribution and transportation FTE personnel of 95 to support increased product throughput at our facilities and to expand our local inventory fulfillment terminals (LIFTs). We had an increase in our remaining FTE personnel of 204 that relates primarily to personnel investments in information technology, manufacturing, and operational support, such as purchasing and product development.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Investing Activities

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Capital expenditures are expected to decrease, but there are signs of strategic investment in IT and distribution, suggesting confidence in future growth.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns have decreased in 2023 compared to 2022, primarily due to reduced dividends and the absence of stock repurchases in the third quarter.

evidence.text: During the first nine months of 2023, we returned $599.5 to our shareholders in the form of dividends, compared to the first nine months of 2022 when we returned $679.0 to our shareholders in the form of dividends ($534.4) and purchases of our common stock ($144.6).
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

evidence.text: During the third quarter of 2023, we returned $199.8 to our shareholders in the form of dividends, compared to the third quarter of 2022 when we returned $272.8 to our shareholders in the form of dividends ($177.5) and purchases of our common stock ($95.3). During the third quarter of 2023, we did not repurchase any of our common stock.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Shareholder returns have decreased due to reduced dividends and no stock repurchases, indicating a negative trend.

factors.summary: The company is improving its debt leverage, as evidenced by a significant reduction in total debt and a decrease in net interest expense. These improvements reflect the company's strategic focus on reducing borrowings and effectively managing its debt levels.

evidence.text: Net cash used in financing activities increased by $372.9 in the first nine months of 2023 when compared to the first nine months of 2022. This is primarily related to a reduction in our debt obligations...
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

evidence.text: Total debt on our balance sheet was $260.0 at the end of the third quarter of 2023, or 7.0% of total capital..., compared to $555.0, or 14.9% of total capital...
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

evidence.text: Our net interest expense was $1.3 in the third quarter of 2023, compared to $3.9 in the third quarter of 2022. We had higher interest income... reflecting higher cash balances... and higher rates paid on those balances. We also had lower interest expense, reflecting lower average borrowings through the period...

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is improving its debt leverage with reduced debt and lower net interest expense, reflecting effective debt management.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, particularly fasteners, which are more exposed to manufacturing end markets. This shift has negatively impacted gross profit percentages due to the lower margins associated with these products.

evidence.text: From a product standpoint, we have three categories: fasteners, safety supplies, and other products... Fasteners are more heavily oriented toward production of final goods than maintenance, which results in greater susceptibility to weaker manufacturing end markets. Second, pricing for fasteners has decelerated at a faster pace than non-fastener products.
Our gross profit percentage was negatively affected by customer and product mix, particularly from Onsite customers and non-fastener products, which have lower gross profit percentages.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, negatively impacting gross profits, which is a concerning trend.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is showing mixed trends. While there is evidence of deceleration in manufacturing end markets, there are also signs of acceleration driven by Onsite locations and key accounts, particularly benefiting manufacturing customers.

evidence.text: We continued to experience a significant divergence in the performance of our manufacturing end market versus our non-manufacturing end markets in the first nine months of 2023. We are growing relatively faster with key account customers, particularly Onsites, with significant managed spend where our service model and technology is particularly impactful, which disproportionately benefits manufacturing customers.

We continued to experience a significant divergence in the performance of our manufacturing end market versus our non-manufacturing end markets in the third quarter of 2023. We are growing relatively faster with key account customers, particularly Onsites, with significant managed spend where our service model and technology is particularly impactful, which disproportionately benefits manufacturing customers.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated some success in raising prices, particularly in the first two quarters of 2023, driven by actions taken in previous quarters to address higher transportation and material costs and inflation. However, the pricing actions have been of modest scope, resulting in mostly stable price levels through the third quarter of 2023, indicating limited ability to raise prices significantly.

evidence.text: The overall impact of product pricing on net sales was 190 to 220 basis points during the first nine months of 2023. While product pricing has been positive throughout 2023, the impact was greatest in the first two quarters of the period. This reflects the carryover of broad actions taken in the first quarter of 2022 and targeted actions taken in the first quarter of 2023 to mitigate the effects of higher transportation and material costs for our products, as well as the impact of general inflationary conditions in the marketplace over the past twelve months.
Incremental pricing actions over the past twelve months have been of modest scope, resulting in mostly stable price levels through the third quarter of 2023.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Pricing power has shown some success but limited ability to significantly raise prices, resulting in a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by Onsite locations and higher daily sales, with notable growth particularly at newer Onsite locations. Despite some softer end markets, the overall trend remains positive, supported by a 5.7% increase in net sales and a 6.3% growth in net daily sales over the first nine months of 2023.

evidence.text: Net sales increased $303.1, or 5.7%, in the first nine months of 2023 when compared to the first nine months of 2022. There was one fewer selling day in the first nine months of 2023 relative to the prior year period and, taking this into consideration, our net daily sales growth increased 6.3% in the first nine months of 2023 compared to the first nine months of 2022. We experienced higher unit sales during the period that contributed to the increase in net sales in the period.
We experienced higher unit sales in the third quarter of 2023 that was primarily due to growth at our Onsite locations, particularly those opened in the last two years.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates strong unit volume growth and positive sales trends, supported by specific percentage increases.

factors.summary: The manufacturing end market is growing faster than the non-manufacturing end market, driven by strong performance with key account customers, particularly Onsites, where the service model and technology have a significant impact.

evidence.text: We continued to experience a significant divergence in the performance of our manufacturing end market versus our non-manufacturing end markets in the first nine months of 2023. We are growing relatively faster with key account customers, particularly Onsites, with significant managed spend where our service model and technology is particularly impactful, which disproportionately benefits manufacturing customers.

We continued to experience a significant divergence in the performance of our manufacturing end market versus our non-manufacturing end markets in the third quarter of 2023. We are growing relatively faster with key account customers, particularly Onsites, with significant managed spend where our service model and technology is particularly impactful, which disproportionately benefits manufacturing customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The manufacturing end market is growing faster, driven by strong performance with key accounts, indicating a positive trend.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, primarily driven by higher employee base pay, healthcare costs, and average wages, despite some offset from lower bonus and commission payments due to slower sales and profit growth.

evidence.text: Our employee-related expenses increased when compared to the first nine months of 2022. We experienced an increase in employee base pay due to higher average FTE and average wages during the period, as well as higher healthcare costs and, to a lesser degree, profit sharing costs. This was only partly offset by a decrease in bonus and commission payments reflecting the impact of slower sales and profit growth versus the prior year.

We experienced an increase in employee base pay due to higher average FTE during the period and, to a lesser degree, higher average wages. We also experienced higher healthcare-related costs. This was partly offset by bonus and commission payments declining to reflect the impact of slower sales and profit growth versus the prior year.
evidence.subsection: Operating Income / Operating and Administrative Expenses

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising labor costs, including base pay and healthcare, despite some offsets, suggest a negative trend.

factors.summary: Input costs are being managed through pricing actions and operational efficiency, with mixed results. While pricing actions have positively impacted net sales, there is evidence of moderating costs, though no recent pricing actions were taken.

evidence.text: The overall impact of product pricing on net sales was 190 to 220 basis points during the first nine months of 2023. While product pricing has been positive throughout 2023, the impact was greatest in the first two quarters of the period. This reflects the carryover of broad actions taken in the first quarter of 2022 and targeted actions taken in the first quarter of 2023 to mitigate the effects of higher transportation and material costs for our products, as well as the impact of general inflationary conditions in the marketplace over the past twelve months.

We experienced slightly positive price-cost. This reflects moderating product costs, as we took no meaningful pricing actions in the period, and an easy comparison, as it largely recaptures the price-cost deficit experienced in the third quarter of 2022.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed results from pricing actions and operational efficiency without recent actions lead to a neutral assessment.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory appears mixed, with some evidence suggesting compression in gross and operating margins due to lower gross profit and higher operating expenses, while other evidence indicates stability in both gross and operating margins when comparing specific quarters.

evidence.text: In the first nine months of 2023, our gross profit, as a percentage of net sales, declined to 45.7% from 46.3% in the first nine months of 2022. The change in our gross profit percentage primarily reflected three items... The operating leverage we achieved during the period was not sufficient to offset the decline in our gross profit percentage.

Our gross profit, as a percentage of net sales, was unchanged at 45.9% in the third quarter of 2023 from 45.9% in the third quarter of 2022. Our operating income, as a percentage of net sales, was unchanged at 21.0% in the third quarter of 2023 from 21.0% in the third quarter of 2022.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Evidence suggests both compression and stability in margins, leading to a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: The supply chain health is showing improvement, driven by the normalization of global supply chains and favorable freight costs. However, slower business activity has somewhat tempered the rate of working capital expansion needed to support customer growth.

evidence.text: This reflects the normalization of global supply chains versus the prior year and, to a lesser degree, slower business activity, which combine to reduce the rate of working capital expansion necessary to support our customers' growth.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

evidence.text: We continue to experience favorable freight costs, which reflects elevated domestic freight revenue leveraging what are relatively stable costs to support our captive fleet, lower expenses related to external freight providers, and lower fuel costs.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in supply chain health due to normalization and favorable freight costs indicates a positive trend.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, driven by operational efficiency, cost management, and technology initiatives. Digital capabilities and freight cost optimizations are key contributors to these gains. However, there are also challenges, such as mix and organizational overhead costs, that partially offset these improvements. Overall, the evidence suggests a mixed but generally positive trend in productivity, with notable contributions from digital transformation and cost-saving measures, though some inefficiencies remain.

evidence.text: The favorable freight expenses partially offset the negative impacts of mix and organizational/overhead costs. This reflected strong domestic freight revenue over the period, which leveraged what are relatively stable costs to support our captive fleet, lower expenses related to external freight providers, and lower fuel costs.

Our Digital Footprint in the third quarter of 2023 represented 57.1% of our sales, an increase from 49.5% of sales in the third quarter of 2022. We believe the data that is created through our digital capabilities enhances product visibility, traceability, and control that reduces risk in operations and creates ordering and fulfillment efficiencies for both ourselves and our customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvements in productivity from operational efficiency and digital transformation, despite some challenges, lead to a positive assessment.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are improving, with a decrease noted in the third quarter of 2023, attributed to the absence of prior supply disruptions. This improvement reflects efficient management with no significant issues of obsolescence or shortage, indicating positive developments in inventory management.

evidence.text: The decrease in our inventory balance in the third quarter of 2023 is primarily attributable to the absence of supply disruptions from the prior year. Our response at the time was to deepen our inventory as a means of maintaining high service to our customers, particularly for imported inventory. Dissipation of these disruptions has allowed us to shorten our product ordering cycle.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities

evidence.text: The text does not specifically address inventory levels.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in inventory levels due to supply disruption absence and efficient management shows improvement.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, as evidenced by higher net cash provided by operating activities and a decrease in cash used for investing and financing activities compared to the prior year.

evidence.text: Cash flow activity was as follows for the periods ended September 30: Net cash provided by operating activities $1,078.7 639.1 68.8 % Percentage of net earnings 121.4 % 76.0 % Net cash used in investing activities $128.2 121.6 5.4 % Percentage of net earnings 14.4 % 14.5 % Net cash used in financing activities $879.1 506.2 73.7 %
Cash flow activity was as follows for the periods ended September 30: Net cash provided by operating activities $388.1 257.9 50.5 %

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: The company faces key sensitivities primarily from foreign exchange movements, which have negatively impacted sales, and economic conditions, including labor cost trends such as higher employee base pay and healthcare-related expenses.

evidence.text: Foreign exchange negatively affected sales in the first nine months of 2023 by approximately 40 basis points.
We estimate that adverse weather in the first quarter of 2023 reduced our growth by approximately 10 basis points during the nine-month period.
Foreign exchange negatively affected sales in the third quarter of 2023 by approximately 10 basis points.
We experienced an increase in employee base pay due to higher average FTE during the period and, to a lesser degree, higher average wages. We also experienced higher healthcare-related costs.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Negative impacts from foreign exchange and economic conditions like labor costs are significant headwinds.

factors.summary: Management is demonstrating confidence through both financial strategies and strategic investments. The reduction in debt and capital expenditures highlights a financially conservative approach, while investments in growth drivers such as Onsite locations and digital capabilities show a forward-looking strategy focused on customer-centric innovation and efficiency.

evidence.text: Total debt on our balance sheet was $260.0 at the end of the third quarter of 2023, or 7.0% of total capital (the sum of stockholders' equity and total debt). This compares to $555.0, or 14.9% of total capital, at the end of the third quarter of 2022. This decrease is due to applying operating cash generated to the reduction of total borrowings on the balance sheet.
evidence.subsection: Net Cash Used in Financing Activities

evidence.text: We are a customer- and growth-centric organization focused on identifying unique technologies, capabilities, and supply chain solutions that get us closer to our customers and reduce the total cost of their global supply chain.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes have had a positive impact on the company's net interest expense. Lower average borrowings and slightly lower average interest rates have contributed to reduced interest expenses, while higher cash balances and higher rates paid on those balances have increased interest income.

evidence.text: Our net interest expense was $7.1 in the first nine months of 2023, compared to $8.9 in the first nine months of 2022. We had higher interest income, reflecting higher cash balances through the period and higher rates paid on those balances. We also had lower interest expense, reflecting lower average borrowings through the period, as well as slightly lower average interest rates.

Our net interest expense was $1.3 in the third quarter of 2023, compared to $3.9 in the third quarter of 2022. We had higher interest income, reflecting higher cash balances through the period and higher rates paid on those balances. We also had lower interest expense, reflecting lower average borrowings through the period, as well as slightly lower average interest rates.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Lower interest expenses and higher income suggest beneficial effects from rate changes and cash management.

factors.summary: The company is effectively differentiating itself through a strong digital footprint and technology-driven services, which have led to significant growth in eCommerce sales and an increased market presence. However, there is no evidence to suggest that this differentiation has translated into a competitive advantage in terms of customer service or supply chain solutions.

evidence.text: Daily sales through eCommerce grew 44.6% in the first nine months of 2023 and represented 23.2% of our total revenues in the period. Our Digital Footprint in the first nine months of 2023 represented 55.5% of our sales, an increase from 48.2% of sales in the first nine months of 2022.

We are a customer- and growth-centric organization focused on identifying unique technologies, capabilities, and supply chain solutions that get us closer to our customers and reduce the total cost of their global supply chain.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2 — MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While digital growth is positive, there's no clear evidence of a competitive advantage in customer service or supply chain.
